title: foundations
purpose: Defines base doctrine for tokens, cascade, naming, theming, layering, and maintainable CSS system design.
when: When creating or refactoring design token systems, defining global styling rules, naming strategy, cascade policy, themes, or dark mode.

title: layout-responsive
purpose: Production rules for layout primitives, spacing systems, breakpoints, fluid design, and responsive consistency.
when: When designing layouts, implementing responsive behavior, establishing breakpoint policy, or creating layout primitives.

title: general-ui-styling
purpose: Rules for day-to-day UI styling: forms, cards, states, controls, density, hierarchy, and surface systems.
when: When styling dashboards, settings pages, cards, forms, tables, modals, filters, or any product UI components.

title: animation-motion
purpose: Tasteful, performance-safe motion for UI content transitions, interactive states, and reduced-motion support.
when: When adding transitions, hover effects, loading animations, panel motion, or any UI animation requiring performance safety.

title: one-pager-landing
purpose: CSS doctrine for landing pages, promo pages, hero sections, CTA styling, and conversion-oriented surfaces.
when: When styling landing pages, marketing pages, hero sections, or conversion-oriented single-page layouts.

title: site-wide-systems
purpose: Rules for styling consistency across large sites, multi-page products, shared themes, and long-term maintenance.
when: When managing CSS across multiple pages or teams, governing tokens, establishing page templates, or ensuring site-wide consistency.

title: css-review-audit
purpose: Structured workflow for reviewing CSS, identifying anti-patterns by severity, and proposing concrete fixes.
when: When auditing existing CSS, reviewing pull requests for styling quality, or identifying and fixing CSS anti-patterns.

title: framework-guides
purpose: Framework-aware guidance for vanilla CSS, SCSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, and CSS-in-JS without mixing rules blindly.
when: When choosing a CSS methodology, applying framework-specific rules, or ensuring proper use of Tailwind, SCSS, CSS Modules, or CSS-in-JS.

title: language-conventions
purpose: Rules for CSS naming, comments, mixed-language teams, and multilingual codebases.
when: When establishing naming conventions, comment standards, or handling CSS in multilingual or mixed-discipline teams.

title: frontend-alchemy
purpose: Create memorable, high-craft frontend interfaces with intentional aesthetic direction, technical correctness, and cohesive restraint.
when: When building frontend interfaces that need to stand out with intentional design, or evaluating design decisions for visual impact.

title: tailwind-design-system
purpose: Build production-ready design systems with Tailwind CSS including tokens, component variants, semantic colors, shadcn/ui patterns, dark mode, and accessibility.
when: When creating Tailwind component libraries, implementing design tokens, building responsive accessible components, or using shadcn/ui.

title: frontend-dev-guidelines
purpose: Production-grade React/TypeScript development with Suspense-first data fetching, feature-based organization, strict TypeScript, and performance-safe defaults.
when: When creating React components, adding features, fetching data, setting up routing, or reviewing frontend code architecture.
